info.title: WorkflowGen CLI
info.description: With the WorkflowGen CLI, you can interact with your WorkflowGen application directly from a terminal or a CI-CD process.

commands.name: action
commands.description: Lets you manage WorkflowGen action (complete, cancel, get)

commands.name: cancel
commands.description: Lets you cancel an ongoing action

arguments.name: actionNumber
arguments.description: Action number of the action which you want to cancel

arguments.name: requestNumber
arguments.description: Id number of the request which you want to cancel the action

commands.name: complete
commands.description: Lets you complete an action for a process

options.name: --parameter
options.description: Your complete action parameter has a string in the following format ValueType:ParameterName=Value. ValueType could be 'text','numeric' or 'date'

arguments.name: activityNumber
arguments.description: Action number of the action which you want to complete

arguments.name: requestNumber
arguments.description: Id number of the request which you want to complete the action

commands.name: get
commands.description: Lets you display the action information of a request or process

options.name: --processName
options.description: Name of the process which you want to retrieve the actions

options.name: --processVersion
options.description: Version of the process which you want to retrieve the actions

options.name: --status
options.description: Retrieve the requests with the status specified, OPEN or CLOSED

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: requestNumber
arguments.description: Id number of request which you want to get

commands.name: application
commands.description: Lets you manage WorkflowGen applications (export, import, get information, delete)

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Lets you delete a single WorkflowGen application by name

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the application which you want to delete

commands.name: export
commands.description: Lets you export a specific application from your WorkflowGen server to a local repository

arguments.name: names
arguments.description: Name(s) of application(s) which you want to export

commands.name: get
commands.description: Lets you display one or several WorkflowGen applications information

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: names
arguments.description: Name(s) of application(s) which you want to get

commands.name: get-parameters
commands.description: Lets you display a WorkflowGen application parameters

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the application which you want to get parameters

commands.name: import
commands.description: Lets you import a specific application to your WorkflowGen server from a local repository

arguments.name: file-path
arguments.description: Path of the application json file

commands.name: config
commands.description: Lets you manage the CLI configuration (context, user, server)

commands.name: current-context
commands.description: Displays the current context

commands.name: delete-context
commands.description: Delete the specified context from the configuration

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Context name to delete

commands.name: delete-server
commands.description: Delete the specified server from the configuration

options.name: --force
options.description: Force the server deletion and delete associated contexts

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Server name to delete

commands.name: delete-user
commands.description: Delete the specified user from the configuration

options.name: --force
options.description: Force the user deletion and delete associated contexts

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: User name to delete

commands.name: get-contexts
commands.description: Describe one or many contexts

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: names
arguments.description: Names of contexts to get

commands.name: get-servers
commands.description: Display servers defined in the configuration

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

commands.name: get-users
commands.description: Display users defined in the configuration

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

commands.name: rename-context
commands.description: Renames a context from the configuration

arguments.name: old-name
arguments.description: Context name to rename

arguments.name: new-name
arguments.description: New context name

commands.name: rename-server
commands.description: Renames a server from the configuration

arguments.name: old-name
arguments.description: Server name to rename

arguments.name: new-name
arguments.description: New server name

commands.name: rename-user
commands.description: Renames a user from the configuration

arguments.name: old-name
arguments.description: User name to rename

arguments.name: new-name
arguments.description: New user name

commands.name: set-context
commands.description: Sets a context entry in the configuration

options.name: --server
options.description: Name of the server which you want to link to the specified context

options.name: --user
options.description: Name of the user which you want to link to the specified context

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the context which you want to set

commands.name: set-server
commands.description: Sets a server entry in configuration

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the server which you want to set

commands.name: set-user
commands.description: Sets a user entry in the configuration

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the user which you want to set

commands.name: use-context
commands.description: Sets the current-context in the configuration

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the context which you want to use

commands.name: folder
commands.description: Lets you manage WorkflowGen process folders (get, list, create, update, delete)

commands.name: create
commands.description: Lets you create a process folder

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the process folder that you want to create

arguments.name: description
arguments.description: Description of the process folder that you want to create

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Lets you delete a single process folder by name

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the process folder that you want to delete

commands.name: get
commands.description: Lets you display one or several process folder' information

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: names
arguments.description: Name of the process folders that you want to get

commands.name: update
commands.description: Lets you update the description for a single process folder

options.name: --description
options.description: New description of the updated process folder

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the process folder that you want to update

commands.name: global-list
commands.description: Lets you manage WorkflowGen global lists (export, import, get information, delete)

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Lets you delete a single global list by name

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the global list which you want to delete

commands.name: export
commands.description: Lets you export a specific global list from your WorkflowGen server to a local repository

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the global list which you want to export

commands.name: get
commands.description: Lets you display one or several global lists information

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: names
arguments.description: Name(s) of global list(s) which you want to get

commands.name: import
commands.description: Lets you import a specific global list from a local repository to your WorkflowGen server

arguments.name: file-path
arguments.description: Path of the global list xml file

commands.name: graphql
commands.description: Lets you execute a custom GraphQl query or mutation

options.name: --variable
options.description: Your GraphQL variable has a string, default format: variable=value

options.name: --variableCast
options.description: Your GraphQL variable cast, default format: variable=value

options.name: --variableJson
options.description: Your GraphQL variable Json input, default format: variable=value

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: Your GraphQL query/mutation

commands.name: login
commands.description: Lets you configure your current WorkflowGen configuration (credentials, server)

options.name: --context
options.description: Name of the context where the configuration will be used. If the context already exists, it will be replaced. Otherwise, it will be created [default: DEFAULT]

options.name: --client-id
options.description: The client id is a public identifier

options.name: --domain
options.description: The domain for ADFS or AUTH0

options.name: --metadata-url
options.description: The meta data URL for OKTA

options.name: --client-secret
options.description: The client secret is a secret known only to the application and the authorization server

options.name: --client-secret-file
options.description: The client secret file is a file containing a client secret known only to the application and the authorization server

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: WorkflowGen Url (ex. https://example.com/wfgen)

commands.name: process
commands.description: Lets you manage WorkflowGen processes (export, import, get information, delete)

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Lets you delete a single process by name and version

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the process which you want to delete

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: Version of the process which you want to delete

commands.name: export
commands.description: Lets you export a specific process from your WorkflowGen server to a local repository

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the process which you want to export

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: Version of the process which you want to export

commands.name: get
commands.description: Lets you display one or several processes

options.name: --folder
options.description: Process folder destination name to import the process, the folder should exist

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the process which you want to get

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: Version of the process which you want to get

commands.name: import
commands.description: Lets you import a specific process from a local repository to your WorkflowGen server

options.name: --use-version-number
options.description: Use the process version from the xml file [default: False]

options.name: --state
options.description: The state of the imported process. It can be DEV, TEST, ACTIVE [default: TEST]

options.name: --folder
options.description: Process folder destination name to import the process, the folder should exist [default: DEFAULT]

arguments.name: file-path
arguments.description: Path of the process xml file

commands.name: test
commands.description: Lets execute test cases

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Set the verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], n[ormal], d[etailed], [default: n]

options.name: --doctor
options.description: Check if test cases are compliants. Only available on 8.0.3+

arguments.name: test-files
arguments.description: Path(s) to test file(s)

commands.name: update
commands.description: Lets you update information for a single process

options.name: --description
options.description: New description of the updated process

options.name: --state
options.description: The state of the updated process. It can be DEV, TEST, ACTIVE, ARCHIVE [default: ]

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the process which you want to update

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: Version of the process which you want to update

commands.name: project
commands.description: Lets you manage a WorkflowGen project (create, export, import)

commands.name: export
commands.description: Lets you export a project from your WorkflowGen server to a local repository

options.name: --path
options.description: Path to the folder where the project's manifest and files are located [default: <path>]

commands.name: import
commands.description: Lets you import a project from a local repository to your WorkflowGen server

options.name: --source
options.description: Path to the folder where the project's manifest and files are located [default: <path>]

options.name: --force
options.description: Replace the existing project global list and application

commands.name: init
commands.description: Lets you initiliaze your project by generating a manifest.json file

options.name: --path
options.description: Directory path where the project will be initialized [default: <path>]

options.name: --bare
options.description: Generate the manifest.json file with the bare minimum content, without prompting for input

commands.name: show
commands.description: Lets you display the content of a manifest link to a project

options.name: --path
options.description: Path to folder where the project's manifest and files are located [default: <path>]

commands.name: request
commands.description: Lets you manage WorkflowGen request (create, cancel, get)

commands.name: cancel
commands.description: Lets you cancel an ongoing request

arguments.name: requestNumber
arguments.description: The request number which you want to cancel

commands.name: create
commands.description: Lets you create a new request from a process

options.name: --parameter
options.description: Your create request parameter has a string in the following format ValueType:ParameterName=Value. ValueType could be 'text','numeric' or 'date'

arguments.name: processName
arguments.description: Name of the process which you want to create a request

arguments.name: processVersion
arguments.description: Version of the process which you want to create a request

commands.name: get
commands.description: Lets you display one or several request information

options.name: --processName
options.description: Name of the process which you want to retrieve requests

options.name: --processVersion
options.description: Version of the process which you want to retrieve requests

options.name: --status
options.description: Retrieve the requests with the status specified, OPEN or CLOSED

options.name: --output
options.description: Define the output format. Valid values: json, table. [default: table]

arguments.name: requestNumbers
arguments.description: Id number(s) of request(s) which you want to get
